codeReview.summary: The feature adds the settings toggle, persistence key, and background trigger wiring for POST/PATCH posts, but the PATCH trigger condition does not actually verify content changes and causes unintended auto-optimization runs.

issues.description: Auto-optimize is triggered whenever `script` or `caption` fields are present in PATCH payload (`parsed.data.script !== undefined || parsed.data.caption !== undefined`), not when their values changed. The editor autosave sends the full form including both fields on blur (`src/components/library/PostEditorDrawer.tsx:51-53`), so unrelated edits (title, notes, status flow) can repeatedly generate duplicate variant posts. This violates the expected behavior that optimization runs on script/caption change.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill requires agent-browser/manual UI verification for UI features, but this worker session did not run agent-browser or dev-server checks and still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` in handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requirement: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md (Required Skills + Manual verification section). Transcript check for worker b783cdbf-7adb-436a-a517-52f52e7d28a7 shows `containsAgentBrowser: false`, `containsDevServerStart: false`, `containsCurl: false`, while handoff verification only lists build/lint.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking correctness issue in PATCH auto-optimize triggering logic that can create repeated unwanted variants on normal autosaves. Shared-state note: skill procedure enforcement appears inconsistent with this worker's verification steps.
